what_is_genuinely_good: prefers-reduced-motion coverage is comprehensive — nearly every animation has a fallback. Rare.
The vibe system is genuinely delightful — shockwave, sparkline, streak counter, ficus growth. Cohesive worldbuilding.
Accessibility is surprisingly solid for a fun project — aria-labels, role attributes, skip-link, keyboard shortcuts, tab roving.
The office visualization is packed with personality — away signs, ghost OOO bubbles, coffee corner chat, door animations.
Tab title cycling and dynamic favicon are great polish details most dashboards never bother with.

suggestions.title: useMemo side-effect in activityLogLenRef
suggestions.description: In App.jsx, stableActivityLog uses useMemo with a side-effect (activityLogLenRef.current = activityLog.length). React docs explicitly warn against side-effects in useMemo — it may skip execution in concurrent mode. Move the ref assignment to a useEffect instead.

suggestions.title: Massive CSS file — Office.css is 1500+ lines
suggestions.description: Office.css is enormous and monolithic. Many animations (@keyframes) are defined but only used conditionally. Consider code-splitting or using CSS modules per component. At minimum, group related blocks and add section comments for maintainability.

suggestions.title: Conference table renders outside visible area on mobile
suggestions.description: ConferenceTable has no explicit positioning (no left/top in CSS class .conference-table). On narrow screens it may overlap with desks or be invisible. Needs position: absolute with responsive coordinates.

suggestions.title: Multiple setInterval timers per Character instance
suggestions.description: Each Character component (manager variant) creates a 45-second ambient quote interval plus hover timeouts. With 6+ characters rendered, this creates multiple concurrent intervals. Consider lifting the ambient broadcast to a single interval in the parent Office component.

suggestions.title: Mobile whiteboard text clips at 480px
suggestions.description: At 480px breakpoint, .office-whiteboard sets font-size: 0 and relies on ::before pseudo-element (which has no content defined in Office.css). The whiteboard appears blank on small screens. Either add content to the ::before or keep a minimum readable font-size.

suggestions.title: Completion toast can overlap error banner
suggestions.description: The '🎉 shipped it!' toast is position:fixed at bottom:2rem with z-index:9999. The fetch-error-banner is at the top. But if multiple toasts fire rapidly (2+ workers ship simultaneously), only the last one shows. Consider a toast queue or stacking.

suggestions.title: Vibe history stored in both localStorage AND sessionStorage
suggestions.description: vibeStreak uses sessionStorage while vibeHistory uses localStorage. The streak resets on new tabs but history persists. This is inconsistent — if streak is meaningful, it should persist across tabs too. Pick one storage strategy.

suggestions.title: Keyboard shortcut '1' and '2' conflict with number input
suggestions.description: The keyboard handler checks for e.target.tagName INPUT/TEXTAREA but not number keys in contentEditable. Also, pressing 'R' to refresh could be confusing — consider requiring a modifier key (Ctrl+R is already browser refresh).

suggestions.title: fetchWorkersFromGitHub cache-busts every request
suggestions.description: Adding ?t=Date.now() defeats GitHub's CDN cache entirely. raw.githubusercontent.com already has short TTLs (~5 min). Remove the cache-buster or use If-None-Match/ETag headers to reduce bandwidth.

suggestions.title: No error recovery UI for individual workers
suggestions.description: When a worker has status 'error', there's a red badge and SOS morse but no way for a viewer to see WHAT the error is. Add an error message field to the worker data and show it in the hover card or a detail panel.

suggestions.title: AwaySign line-splitting logic is fragile
suggestions.description: The reduce-based line splitter in AwaySign puts only the first word on line1 and everything else on line2. For 'Taking a lap' → line1='Taking', line2='a lap'. This works by accident but isn't intentional word-wrapping. Use a simpler split approach.

suggestions.title: Light theme barely tested — several hardcoded dark colors
suggestions.description: Office.css has a few [data-theme='light'] overrides but most colors (floor, monitor, particles) use hardcoded dark values. The light theme works but feels like an afterthought. Need a pass to make light mode feel intentional.

suggestions.title: No notification/sound when workers ship
suggestions.description: The completion toast and confetti are visual-only. Consider an optional browser notification or subtle sound effect when a worker finishes. Could be toggled in settings.

suggestions.title: CharacterAvatar has growing if/else chain for worker IDs
suggestions.description: Each worker (bloberto, carlos, maya, dave, sofia, luna) has a dedicated SVG block in CharacterAvatar. This doesn't scale — adding a new team member means adding 30+ lines of JSX. Consider a data-driven approach with SVG templates.
